hero.headline: One key. One wallet. Everything an agent needs.
hero.subhead: AISA is the transaction network for the AI agent economy: a 9-provider model gateway, ~650 data and action endpoints, and machine payments behind a single key. Right now the front door is broken (the /models page renders zero models) and there are ~0 named public builders behind a "5,000+ agents" claim. I fix that funnel before flooding it — I built the developer program at Cisco Meraki, led DevRel at MetaMask, and bring an ~86k-developer community as a day-one attributed channel.

plan.north_star: Weekly Active Agents making multi-capability calls — a model route plus at least one data-API family in the same period — payment-weighted once x402 is GA. Not signups, not followers.

site_releases.name: Front Door
site_releases.goal: Stop losing every visitor at the door: an honest, working landing surface that a builder can trust in 60 seconds.
ships: Unbreak /models (fix the tRPC + pricing 404 so it renders real, live model counts, not "0 models")
Kill inflated claims — one honest number replaces "1000+ LLMs" and the unverifiable "5,000+ agents"
Publish the candid self-review page: the broken page and not-yet-GA payments stated plainly as the trust engine
Instrument the funnel end-to-end (visit -> key -> first call) so Activation is measurable from day one

site_releases.name: Golden Path
site_releases.goal: First real API call in under 5 minutes, framework-native, agent-native.
ships: One-prompt agent-native onboarding (llms.txt, per-page .md, A2A card, copy-paste prompt)
OpenAI-compatible base_url quickstart proving the drop-in swap in one snippet
Four framework quickstarts: OpenClaw, Claude Code, Cursor, Hermes
Live "time to first call" dashboard tied to the north-star instrumentation

site_releases.name: The Wedge Aha
site_releases.goal: Show the thing OpenRouter and Vercel AI Gateway cannot: capabilities beyond tokens, in one flow.
ships: Flagship 10-min tutorial: build a GTM agent using model + Apollo + DataForSEO behind one key
Three vertical sample apps spanning agent-builder, quant/fintech, and GTM-automation audiences
Capability catalog page mapping all 14 families / ~650 endpoints to real agent jobs
First named public builders shipped and attributed as case studies

site_releases.name: Distribution Flywheel
site_releases.goal: Make AISA the default install and the answer search returns.
ships: Official TypeScript and Python SDKs with agent-first ergonomics
Framework plugins so AISA is one line in the tools most agent builders already use
SEO + comparison cluster (vs OpenRouter, vs Vercel AI Gateway) built on the honesty angle
41 packaged skills (OpenClaw / Claude Code / Hermes) published as a browsable, installable gallery

site_releases.name: Community & Launch Moment
site_releases.goal: Turn scattered users into a visible, self-referring community with one coordinated moment.
ships: Discord plumbing, support loop, and public builder showcase wired to the north star
Coordinated launch moment amplified through the ~86k-developer dabl.club channel
Weekly Active Agents reported publicly as the scoreboard

site_releases.name: The Moat Goes Public
site_releases.goal: Ship the wedge that no gateway can copy: autonomous machine payments, live.
ships: x402 / HTTP-402 payments GA with Circle Nanopayments and a clean free-to-paid path
Prediction-market arbitrage capstone using Polymarket + Kalshi + payments as the flagship demo
Payment-weighted north-star reporting switched on

north_star: Weekly Active Agents making multi-capability calls — a model route plus at least one data-API family in the same period — payment-weighted once x402 is GA. Not signups, not followers.

sections.heading: Real product. No developer brand. Broken front door.
sections.body_md:
AISA.one already ships the hard part: one API key that fronts a 9-provider model gateway plus ~650 data and action endpoints across 14 families, plus 41 packaged skills, with machine-to-machine payments wired in behind it. The substrate is real and unusually deep. What is missing is everything that turns a substrate into an ecosystem.

There is no visible DevRel motion, no community loop, and the flagship `/models` page renders "0 models" because the tRPC and pricing fetch 404s. The site claims "5,000+ agents" but there are ~0 named public builders anyone can point to. That is the gap: not more surface area, but a working front door and a reason for builders to stay. Fix the funnel before flooding it. Kill the vanity numbers, unbreak the page, ship one honest 10-minute path to a real agent, then measure the only thing that matters.

North star: Weekly Active Agents making multi-capability calls, a model route plus at least one data-API family in the same period, payment-weighted once x402 is GA. Not signups, not followers.

cards.title: What works
cards.label: SURFACE AREA
cards.detail: One key fronts the model gateway, the ~650-endpoint data/action catalog, and 41 skills. Three products that are secretly one substrate: everything an autonomous agent needs behind one key and one wallet.

cards.title: What's broken
cards.label: THE FRONT DOOR
cards.detail: The flagship /models page renders "0 models" (tRPC + pricing fetch 404/fail). The honest catalog is buried behind a page that tells every visitor the product is empty.

cards.title: What's missing
cards.label: THE LOOP
cards.detail: No visible DevRel motion, no community loop, ~0 named public builders. "5,000+ agents" is a number no one can verify or credit to a real developer.

cards.title: What DevRel closes
cards.label: THE GAP
cards.detail: Unbreak /models, replace vanity numbers with one honest metric, ship a 10-minute build-a-real-agent path, and instrument the funnel toward Weekly Active Agents making multi-capability calls.

sections.title: The setup

sections.heading: Three products that are secretly one substrate
sections.body_md:
Cheap model routing is a commodity. OpenRouter and Vercel AI Gateway already won the "same base_url, lower token price" game, and the margins prove it. AISA is not really in that fight. The gateway is table stakes. What sits behind the same key is the actual product: ~650 data and action endpoints across 14 families, 41 packaged skills, and machine-to-machine payments. An autonomous agent needs a model, live data, an action to take, and a way to pay for it. AISA is the only one of the three that puts all four behind one key and one wallet.

That is the wedge. A gateway routes tokens. AISA routes capabilities plus settlement. The moment an agent makes a model call *and* pulls an Apollo lead list *and* enriches it with DataForSEO in the same session, switching cost stops being a base_url swap and becomes a rewrite of the agent's entire capability graph. The north-star metric is built on exactly this behavior: Weekly Active Agents making multi-capability calls, payment-weighted once x402 is GA. Not signups. Not followers.

The honest caveat up front: the payment layer (x402, Circle Nanopayments, MPP) is Private Beta, and Foundry is Coming Soon. So today the moat is capabilities-beyond-tokens and agent-native distribution. The payments moat goes public later, on purpose.

cards.label: MOAT 01
cards.title: Capabilities, not tokens
cards.detail: A gateway sells you a cheaper model. AISA sells you what the agent does with it: 445 DataForSEO endpoints, 54 Apollo, 46 AgentMail, 32 Twitter, plus Financial, CoinGecko, Polymarket, Kalshi. That catalog is the reason to switch, and it has no equivalent on OpenRouter or Vercel AI Gateway.

cards.label: MOAT 02
cards.title: One key, one wallet
cards.detail: 9-provider model gateway + 650 data/action APIs + M2M payments behind a single credential. Competitors give you a routing key and stop there. Every additional capability an agent wires up deepens lock-in, because the alternative is stitching a dozen vendor accounts and billing relationships by hand.

cards.label: MOAT 03
cards.title: Autonomous settlement
cards.detail: x402 / HTTP-402, Circle Nanopayments, and MPP let an agent pay per call without a human in the loop. This is the part no AI gateway has. It is Private Beta today, which is why payments GTM is sequenced last, but it is what turns a capability network into a transaction network.

cards.label: MOAT 04
cards.title: Agent-native distribution
cards.detail: llms.txt, per-page .md doc mirrors, an A2A agent card, and one-prompt onboarding mean an agent can discover and integrate AISA without a human reading docs. The buyer is the agent. That is a distribution surface a token router built for humans does not have.

sections.title: The real wedge

sections.heading: Sharpened from using the product
sections.body_md:
I ran AISA the way a builder would before I ran it the way a proposal would. One key, the OpenAI-compatible base_url, a model route, and a couple of data-API families in the same session. Some of it is genuinely good. Some of it is broken right now, and I'm going to say which is which.

Here's the operating principle for this whole engagement: publish the review even where AISA loses today. The broken `/models` page and the not-yet-GA payments are stated plainly below. Candor is the trust engine. A DevRel motion that hides the funnel it's trying to fix has already lost the room it's trying to win.

cards.label: THE BUG / DAY-ONE FIX
cards.title: /models renders "0 models"
cards.detail: The flagship discovery page is a P0. The tRPC call plus pricing fetch 404s and the grid falls back to zero, so the first thing a builder sees on a 9-provider gateway is an empty catalog. This is the top of the funnel and it's dark. v0.1 unbreaks it, kills the "1000+ LLMs" claim for one honest count, and instruments the page so we know what conversion actually is. Nothing gets flooded before the front door works.

cards.label: THE POSITIONING INSIGHT
cards.title: The wedge isn't cheap tokens
cards.detail: Model routing is a commodity; competing with OpenRouter and Vercel AI Gateway on price is a losing seat. What's actually rare is what sits behind the same key: ~650 data/action endpoints across 14 families plus autonomous M2M payments. The three products aren't three products, they're one substrate. The 10-minute flagship should prove it by wiring a model route to Apollo plus DataForSEO in a single agent. That's the demo nobody else can run.

cards.label: THE EDGE
cards.title: Depth the catalog already has
cards.detail: DataForSEO at 445 endpoints, Apollo at 54, plus Financial, CoinGecko, Polymarket, and Kalshi is an unusually deep bench for quant/fintech and GTM-automation builders. That's a real, present advantage, not a roadmap promise. The gap is distribution, not surface area: "5,000+ agents" claimed with roughly zero named public builders. Turning that catalog into named, shipping agents is the entire opportunity.

sections.title: Sharpened from using the product

sections.heading: Six programs, run in sequence. Each one attacks a specific lever.
sections.body_md:
These are not parallel bets. They ship in roadmap order because the order is the strategy: fix the funnel before flooding it. You cannot run a launch moment on top of a `/models` page that renders "0 models," and you cannot sell payments GTM before M2M is near-GA. Every initiative maps to one arc and one lever, and every one moves toward the same north star: **Weekly Active Agents making multi-capability calls** (a model route plus at least one data-API family in the same period), payment-weighted once x402 is GA. Not signups, not followers.

Each card below states what it fixes, what ships, and how we know it worked. Where the product loses today (broken flagship page, payments not GA), we say so and sequence around it rather than paper over it.

cards.title: 01 / Front Door
cards.label: v0.1 — Lever: stop the leak
cards.detail: Unbreak /models (the tRPC + pricing fetch that 404s and renders "0 models"), kill the "1000+ LLMs" and "5,000+ agents" claims, replace them with one honest number, and instrument the funnel end to end. Nothing else ships until a builder landing on the site can see what's real. Signal: /models renders correct provider + endpoint counts; funnel events firing from key issuance to first call.

cards.title: 02 / Golden Path
cards.label: v0.2 — Lever: time-to-first-call
cards.detail: Agent-native one-prompt onboarding on top of the existing llms.txt / per-page .md / A2A card surface, plus 4 framework quickstarts (OpenClaw, Claude Code, Cursor, Hermes). The one drop-in that has to land: OpenAI-compatible base_url, first successful call in under 5 minutes. Signal: median time-to-first-call and quickstart completion rate per framework.

cards.title: 03 / The Wedge Aha
cards.label: v0.3 — Lever: multi-capability activation
cards.detail: The flagship 10-minute tutorial: build a real GTM agent using a model route + Apollo + DataForSEO in one key, one wallet. Plus 3 vertical sample apps proving the wedge (capabilities beyond tokens), aimed at the deep quant/fintech + GTM catalog. This is the initiative that directly manufactures the north-star event: model route + >=1 data-API family in the same period. Signal: share of active agents making multi-capability calls.

cards.title: 04 / Distribution Flywheel
cards.label: v0.5 — Lever: reach + repeat usage
cards.detail: TypeScript and Python SDKs, framework plugins, and an SEO / comparison content cluster positioned honestly against OpenRouter and Vercel AI Gateway (not on price — on capabilities + payments + agent-native distribution). The dabl.club ~86k-developer community (36-40% open rate) turns on here as a day-one attributed acquisition channel. Signal: SDK installs, plugin adoption, organic + attributed activation.

cards.title: 05 / Community & Launch Moment
cards.label: v1.0 — Lever: retention + awareness
cards.detail: Discord plumbing, a public builder showcase (the fix for "~0 named public builders"), and one coordinated launch. This only fires after the funnel is fixed and the wedge is proven, because a launch amplifies whatever state the front door is in. Signal: named public builders shipped, weekly-active retention, launch-window activation.

cards.title: 06 / The Moat Goes Public
cards.label: v1.3 — Lever: payment-weighted value
cards.detail: x402 payments GA becomes the GTM: a prediction-market arb capstone (Polymarket + Kalshi + Financial data + a model route + autonomous settlement) and a clean free-to-paid path. Payment-weighting switches on in the north star here, not before. Sets up v2.0 self-sustaining: Foundry GA, ambassador program, two-sided capability catalog. Signal: payment-weighted Weekly Active Agents, free-to-paid conversion.

table: Share of agents making multi-capability calls
sections.title: The initiatives

sections.heading: Seven releases from broken front door to self-sustaining program
sections.body_md:
This is a DevRel release ladder, not a product one. We fix the funnel before we flood it: unbreak `/models`, publish one honest number, ship a 10-minute "build a real agent" tutorial, then earn the right to run a payments GTM once M2M is near-GA. Each rung has one primary KPI and one exit gate. You do not climb until the gate clears.

Every KPI rolls up to the north star: Weekly Active Agents making multi-capability calls (a model route plus at least one data-API family in the same period), payment-weighted once x402 is GA. Not signups, not followers.
table: Unbreak /models, kill "1000+ LLMs" for one honest number, instrument the funnel
/models renders real inventory; funnel events firing end to end
Dev surface up <72h and stable; visitor -> first-key drop-off measurable
Agent-native one-prompt onboarding, first call in 5 min, 4 framework quickstarts
New builder makes a first successful call in under 5 min; 4 quickstarts live
Flagship "build a GTM agent in 10 min" (model + Apollo + DataForSEO); 3 vertical sample apps
Multi-capability activation rate (model route + >=1 data family)
Pilot activation >=20%; tutorial completers make a real multi-capability call
TS + Python SDKs, framework plugins, SEO / comparison content cluster
SDKs shipped and installed by named builders; comparison cluster ranking and driving keys
Discord plumbing + public showcase + one coordinated launch
Named public builders in the showcase; WAA lift through launch window
First named-builder showcase live; community loop retaining week over week
x402 payments GA, prediction-market arb capstone, free -> paid conversion
Paying agents transacting via x402; capstone reproducible by an outside builder
Foundry GA, ambassador program, two-sided capability catalog
Program runs without the fractional lead in the loop
Ambassadors and catalog contributors driving activation; growth not dependent on one operator

cards.label: Sequencing rule
cards.title: Fix the funnel before you flood it
cards.detail: P0 is not awareness. It is the broken flagship /models page (renders "0 models": tRPC + pricing fetch 404/fail), the "1000+ LLMs" claim traded for one number that is true, and a funnel you can actually read. Payments GTM waits for v1.3, when M2M is near-GA. Marketing a broken front door just spends attention you can't get back.

cards.label: One gate that repeats
cards.title: Activation, or we don't climb
cards.detail: The recurring exit gate across the ladder is real multi-capability activation, tied to the SOW kill-switch: pilot activation below 20% means no renewal. Every rung has to move an agent from a signup to a model-plus-data call, then to a paid one. Codenames are memory aids; the KPI column is the contract.

sections.title: Roadmap to v2

sections.heading: One north star, and the funnel that feeds it
sections.body_md:
The north star is **Weekly Active Agents making multi-capability calls** — a model route plus at least one data-API family in the same period, payment-weighted once x402 is GA. Not signups. Not followers. An agent that only hits the gateway is a commodity token user; an agent that pairs a model route with Apollo, DataForSEO, or Financial data is living on the substrate, and that is the only number worth defending.

Everything upstream is instrumented to move that one number. Each stage below has a KPI you cannot fake with a press release. Vanity counts ("5,000+ agents," "1000+ LLMs") are explicitly out — the first program deliverable is killing them and publishing honest ones. Awareness and Advocacy are assist stages; the engagement owns Activation end to end.

Real builders with real traffic, not testimonials or logos
sections.title: Metrics that matter

sections.heading: Where AISA wins, where it bleeds, and what's actually contestable
sections.body_md:
The honest read against OpenRouter and Vercel AI Gateway. Both of those are excellent at cheap, fast model routing, and that is a commodity race AISA should not run. The defensible ground is everything past the token: ~650 data and action endpoints across 14 families, autonomous M2M payments, and agent-native distribution, all behind one key and one wallet. The catch is that the front door is broken today and there are ~0 named public builders, so the strengths are latent until the funnel is fixed.

Read each quadrant as a claim we would stand behind in public, including the parts where AISA loses right now.

cards.label: STRENGTHS
cards.title: Capabilities beyond tokens, one key
cards.detail: Real surface area competitors do not have: 9-provider OpenAI-compatible gateway PLUS ~650 data/action endpoints across 14 families (DataForSEO 445, Apollo 54, AgentMail 46, Twitter 32, Financial 22) PLUS 41 packaged skills. Gateway, data APIs, and skills are secretly one substrate.

cards.label: STRENGTHS
cards.title: Payments as a moat, not a feature
cards.detail: Machine-to-machine settlement via x402/HTTP-402, Circle Nanopayments, and MPP. OpenRouter and Vercel AI Gateway route tokens; neither lets an agent pay for arbitrary capabilities autonomously. This is the part of the story a competitor cannot copy in a sprint.

cards.label: STRENGTHS
cards.title: Agent-native by construction
cards.detail: llms.txt, per-page .md doc mirrors, an A2A agent card, and one-prompt onboarding. The product is legible to the agents that are meant to consume it, not just to humans reading marketing pages.

cards.label: STRENGTHS
cards.title: Day-one distribution channel
cards.detail: Seller brings an ~86k-developer community (dabl.club, 36-40% open rate) as an attributed acquisition channel, plus DevRel leadership from Cisco Meraki and MetaMask. Distribution is usually the hardest DevRel cold-start; here it exists on signing.

cards.label: WEAKNESSES
cards.title: Broken flagship /models page
cards.detail: The /models page renders '0 models' (tRPC + pricing fetch 404/fail). The single highest-intent page for an evaluating builder is dead. This is P0: unbreak it before any traffic is driven anywhere.

cards.label: WEAKNESSES
cards.title: No named public builders
cards.detail: '5,000+ agents' is claimed but there are ~0 named public builders and no showcase. The proof layer that turns a curious dev into a committed one does not exist yet.

cards.label: WEAKNESSES
cards.title: No DevRel motion, no community loop
cards.detail: No visible developer relations activity, no Discord plumbing, no feedback loop. Real surface area with no one narrating it, staffing it, or closing the loop with the people using it.

cards.label: WEAKNESSES
cards.title: Credibility-eroding numbers
cards.detail: Inflated claims (e.g. '1000+ LLMs') undercut trust with exactly the skeptical builder audience AISA needs. One honest number beats ten aspirational ones for this crowd.

cards.label: OPPORTUNITIES
cards.title: Own the wedge nobody else can
cards.detail: 'Cheap model routing' is a commodity OpenRouter and Vercel already win. Capabilities-beyond-tokens + autonomous payments is uncontested. A 10-min 'build a GTM agent' tutorial (model + Apollo + DataForSEO) demonstrates the wedge in one sitting.

cards.label: OPPORTUNITIES
cards.title: Unusually deep verticals
cards.detail: The Apollo / DataForSEO / Financial / Polymarket / Kalshi catalog is deep enough to court quant/fintech and GTM-automation builders directly. A prediction-market arbitrage capstone is a story no gateway competitor can tell.

cards.label: OPPORTUNITIES
cards.title: First-mover on x402 GA
cards.detail: Once M2M payments hit GA, AISA can define the agent-payments narrative for the crypto/x402 builder segment before the category is crowded. Sequencing matters: payments GTM only once it is near-GA.

cards.label: OPPORTUNITIES
cards.title: Agent-native SEO/discovery
cards.detail: A comparison and SEO cluster plus SDKs (TS + Python) and framework plugins for OpenClaw/Claude Code/Cursor/Hermes turn agent-native docs into a compounding distribution flywheel.

cards.label: THREATS
cards.title: Commodity gateways expanding
cards.detail: OpenRouter and Vercel AI Gateway have distribution and mindshare today. If either bolts on a credible data/action or payments layer, the window on AISA's wedge narrows. Speed to the aha moment is the defense.

cards.label: THREATS
cards.title: The funnel stays broken
cards.detail: If /models and honest numbers are not fixed first, any awareness spend leaks straight out. Flooding a broken funnel manufactures churn, not activation. Kill-switch: dev-surface broken >72h pauses the engagement.

cards.label: THREATS
cards.title: Payments slip past GA
cards.detail: The strongest moat (x402/M2M) is not yet GA. If the timeline slips, the differentiated story stays theoretical and AISA competes on the commodity axis it should avoid.

cards.label: THREATS
cards.title: Activation fails to convert
cards.detail: If builders sign up but never make a multi-capability call, the north-star flatlines regardless of top-of-funnel. Kill-switch: pilot activation <20% means no renewal. The metric is honest by design.

sections.title: SWOT

sections.heading: Fractional Head of DevRel. Priced in hours, killable in 30 days.
sections.body_md:
This is a fractional SOW, not a headcount req. One operator owns **Activation** end to end (first call to Weekly Active Agent) and assists on Awareness. Rate is flat $150/hr across all tiers; the tiers differ only in duration and weekly load. **Standard is the recommendation:** one month is enough to unbreak the front door but not to prove the funnel holds; six months is a bet you shouldn't make before v0.2 ships. Three months buys the full v0.1-to-v0.3 arc (front door fixed, golden path live, the model-plus-Apollo-plus-DataForSEO wedge demo published) with a real activation signal to renew or walk on.

Every tier ships against the north star: **Weekly Active Agents making multi-capability calls** (a model route plus at least one data-API family in the same period), payment-weighted once x402 is GA. Not signups, not followers. If that number doesn't move, the kill-switches below make it cheap to stop.

Scope is deliberately narrow. In: fixing `/models`, one honest number to replace "1000+ LLMs," the 10-minute flagship tutorial, quickstarts, funnel instrumentation, and the day-one dabl.club acquisition channel. Out: paid acquisition, conference sponsorships, payments GTM before M2M is near-GA, and anything that floods a funnel that isn't fixed yet.
table: Unbreak /models, one honest number, instrument the funnel (v0.1)
v0.1 through v0.3: front door, golden path, the wedge aha demo + vertical samples
Through v1.0: SDKs, plugins, Discord, community, coordinated launch moment

cards.label: OWNED
cards.title: Activation, end to end
cards.detail: First call to Weekly Active Agent making multi-capability calls. Includes the /models fix, honest numbers, the 10-min build-a-real-agent flagship, 4 framework quickstarts, and funnel instrumentation.

cards.label: ASSIST
cards.title: Awareness
cards.detail: dabl.club (~86k developers, 36-40% open rate) as a day-one attributed channel plus SEO/comparison content. Assist, not own: no paid media, no conference budget line.

cards.label: NON-GOALS
cards.title: What this is not
cards.detail: Not payments GTM before M2M is near-GA. Not a launch splash on a broken funnel. Not vanity-metric reporting. Not full-time headcount. Not owning product, sales, or support.

cards.label: KILL-SWITCHES
cards.title: Cheap to stop
cards.detail: Pilot activation under 20% at term end: no renew. Dev surface (e.g. /models) broken more than 72h: engagement pauses and prorates. 30-day mutual notice, any time, any tier.

stats.label: Flat rate, all tiers
stats.sub: Tiers differ by term and load, not price

stats.label: Pilot activation kill line
stats.sub: Below it at term end, no renewal

sections.title: Engagement

footer: AISA DevRel strategy — an outside-in proposal for the transaction network of the AI agent economy. Fix the funnel before you flood it.
